body.title: [hold] Repo auditor: evaluate whether semantic layer adds precision
body.result: structural auditor returns 0 flags: get_closed_tickets() returns size=None for all tickets; no M/L/XL tickets in audit set to eval against. Fix get_closed_tickets() size field first.
body.intention: I intend that the semantic (embedding cosine similarity) layer is only wired into the auditor if it demonstrably improves flag precision over structural-only — not on faith.
body.description:
After T-repo-auditor-structural ships and produces its first real flag list, run a precision eval:

1. Take the top-30 structural flags from the first run
2. Manually review 10 of them (sample) to establish a ground-truth precision baseline for structural-only
3. For the same 30 tickets: embed ticket description + commit summary using the existing embedding pipeline; compute cosine similarity; compare structural scores vs structural+semantic scores against the ground-truth labels
4. Report: does semantic add >= 10% precision lift? If yes → file T-repo-auditor-semantic-wire. If no → close the semantic thread with a note.

**Affected files:** lab/competition/ (read: reuse embedding pipeline); devices/hubert/repo_auditor.py (read)
**Design rules:** none — this is an eval script, not production code
**Scope boundary:** IN — eval script + precision measurement. OUT — wiring semantic into the nightly run (only if eval passes).
**Completion criteria:** Eval script runs end-to-end; produces a one-screen report showing structural-only precision vs structural+semantic precision on the 10-sample ground truth; verdict (wire / don't wire) written to `hubert.audit_flags` metadata field.
